commit.message:
Add node feature normalization for stable GNN training

Normalizes features: x = (x - mean) / (std + 1e-6)
Prevents unstable gradients from raw pixel values.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add edge_index out-of-bounds validation

Prevents crash when edge_index.max() >= num_nodes in PyG graphs.

commit.message:
[paper] Paper 4: fix sub-resonant TF to relative displacement (Reviewer A R2) (#169)

- Code: H = r^2/|D(r)| (relative) instead of 1/|D(r)| (absolute)
- Text: fix H description from 'H ≈ 1.15' to relative ratio r^2/|D| ≈ 0.22
- Regenerate fig_sub_resonant_response with correct relative displacements
- Values now consistent: ~160 um sub-resonant, ~47 um at-resonance, 3-4x ratio
- 31 bladder tests + 206 repo tests pass

[paper] Paper 4: major revision fixes (fill-dependent reframe, claim separation, figure upgrade) (#163)

- Reframe narrative around U-shaped f₂(V) fill-dependent physics as central novelty
- Separate model predictions / biological hypotheses / clinical conjecture into 3 tiers
- Elevate sub-resonant forced response analysis to prominent results section with new figure
- Upgrade all figures to publication quality (300 DPI, consistent fonts, professional palette)

- Update title, abstract, highlights, keywords, conclusion

commit.message:
[paper] Paper 3: major revision fixes (circularity reframe, uncertainty propagation, sphere error bound) (#162)

- Reframe Pi_0 constancy as consequence of assumed input constancy, not independent finding
- Add formal uncertainty propagation appendix (±20% in E,h -> ~4% Pi_0 CV, cf. 4.5% inter-species)
- Bound equivalent-sphere approximation error at O(e^2) = 50% with new Discussion section
- Justify R_scat as power-scattering cross-section ratio, cite Morse & Ingard and Pierce
- Rename Parametric validation -> Parametric verification, clarify algebraic identity
- State cross-species parameters as illustrative assumptions, not measured data
- Note nu=0.45 held constant eliminates one governing group
- Qualify 1/a scaling remark: h/a varies 1.34x across species
- State K_f/E = 2.2e4 ratio explicitly for breathing-mode justification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add video format support and improve date fallback accuracy

- Added .mp4, .mov, .avi, .mkv to scan extensions (renamed variable
  from ImageExtensions to MediaExtensions)
- Added fileSystemInfo to \ in search query so device creation
  date is available for videos and photos without EXIF
- Date priority is now: EXIF takenDateTime > fileSystemInfo.createdDateTime
  (when the file was created on the device/camera) > createdDateTime
  (OneDrive upload date — least accurate, used as last resort)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add prisma singleton + withRetry to eliminate transient DB errors

- Add server/src/utils/prisma.ts: singleton PrismaClient + withRetry() that
  auto-reconnects on P1001/P1008/P1017/P2024/ECONNRESET errors
- Wrap all DB calls in every controller with withRetry()
- Add directUrl = env(DIRECT_URL) to schema.prisma for pgBouncer support
- All controllers now share one PrismaClient instance (no more connection
  storms from multiple new PrismaClient() calls)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix parser error: replace for-loop cast with range pipeline

PowerShell cannot cast a 'for' statement as an expression. Replace
with '0..n | ForEach-Object' which is a valid pipeline expression and
correctly increments the index via \.

commit.message:
[paper] Paper 5: fix radial equation, mode dominance, scaling law (#150)

- Rederive radial breathing mode with per-displacement stiffness
  (k_gas = 2γP₀/R, k_wall = Eh/[R²(1-ν²)]): 1329 Hz → 163 Hz
- Correct axial sub-audibility threshold (1 mL → ~10 mL)
- Add axial competition acknowledgement for small volumes (V < 2.5 mL)
- Fix V⁻¹ → V⁻¹/² scaling in mode selection and discussion
- Acknowledge Q=4 vs 500-800 ms burst-duration inconsistency
  (ring-down ~18 ms; borborygmi are sustained excitation events)
- Reframe Minnaert as limiting case, not separate mechanism (5 → 4)
- Add regression tests for corrected radial, axial scaling, threshold

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(binding): COMBAT_AND_LOG upstream owner patches (issue #201) (#209)

* feat(binding): COMBAT_AND_LOG upstream owner patches (issue #201)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(review): handle singular "is stuck" in GameObjectMoveTranslationPatch

Address Codex P2 finding: IsTargetMessage now matches both "is stuck." and
"are stuck." to cover singular entities from Does("are") conjugation.
Adds L2 test for the singular case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(review): address CodeRabbit findings — Finalizer, ShouldTrack, warning match

- Replace Postfix with Finalizer across all 14 context patches for
  exception safety (Harmony 2.x Postfix skips on exception)
- GameObjectRegeneraTranslationPatch: evaluate ShouldTrack once in
  Prefix, store result in ThreadStatic to avoid duplicate reflection
  on the hot FireEvent path
- ZoneManagerTickTranslationPatch: accept both "&R"-embedded and
  plain text + color="R" parameter forms of the warning message

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(review): use Stack<bool> for reentrant FireEvent tracking

Address CodeRabbit Critical: lastPrefixTracked single bool is overwritten
on nested FireEvent calls, causing activeDepth leak. Replace with
Stack<bool> to properly track each call frame.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[infra] Create Dietrich — emeritus vibroacoustics collaborator agent (#141)

The loving spouse kept saying 'talk to Dietrich.' Now he exists.

Dietrich Hartmann is a retired ISVR professor with 40 years of structural
acoustics expertise. He provides deep scientific collaboration — physical
intuition, literature guidance, and JSV framing advice. Complements the
coffee-machine-guru (meta-research) with substantive physics discussion.

Also updates the loving-spouse agent to distinguish between Dietrich
(@dietrich for science) and the coffee machine (@coffee-machine-guru
for confidence).

commit.message:
chore: update visual regression snapshots (#1044)

## Summary

Snapshot changes detected by the \`R-CMD-check-hard\` workflow (Linux, R
release) on 2026-03-29.

The following test suites have updated SVG snapshots (66 files total):

- \`ggbarstats\` (6 files)
- \`ggbetweenstats\` (5 files)
- \`ggcoefstats\` (8 files)
- \`ggcorrmat\` (2 files)
- \`ggdotplotstats\` (2 files)
- \`gghistostats\` (3 files)
- \`ggpiestats\` (10 files)
- \`ggscatterstats\` (4 files)
- \`ggwithinstats\` (5 files)
- \`pairwise-ggsignif\` (21 files)

## Root Cause of CI Failures on `oldrel`

The \`R-CMD-check\` workflow was failing on **R 4.4.3** (`oldrel`) but
passing on **R 4.5.3** (`release`) and **R 4.3.x** (`oldrel-2`).

**Why `oldrel-2` (R 4.3) passed:** Tests were already skipped via `if
(getRversion() >= "4.4.0")` — so R 4.3 never ran the snapshot tests.

**Why `oldrel` (R 4.4) failed but `release` (R 4.5) passed:** Both R
4.4.3 and R 4.5.3 were using the **same ggplot2 version (4.0.2)**, so
the cause is **not** a ggplot2 update. The difference is in R's own
graphics engine between R 4.4 and R 4.5 — specifically, SVG text
elements have slightly different y-coordinates (e.g., `y='20.88'` in R
4.4 vs `y='21.11'` in R 4.5, a ~0.23px shift). The snapshots in this PR
were regenerated on R 4.5.3, making them incompatible with R 4.4's
renderer.

**Fix:** Updated `tests/testthat.R` to require `getRversion() >=
"4.5.0"` (previously `>= "4.4.0"`). This mirrors the original rationale
— when R's graphics engine changes in a way that affects snapshot
rendering, the version gate is bumped to match where the snapshots were
generated.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[paper] Paper 2: re-derive constrained-bubble equation from Church model

Fix fatal flaw: old per-volume-change formulation (K_gas=3gP0, divided by
a^2) did not recover the Minnaert limit when wall terms vanished.

Replace with per-displacement formulation (Church 1995 / Hoff 2001):
  k_gas  = 3*gamma*P0 / a        (gas stiffness per unit radial disp)
  k_wall = 2*E*h / (a^2*(1-nu))  (shell hoop stiffness)
  m_fluid = rho_f * a             (radiation added mass)
  m_wall  = rho_w * h             (shell inertia)
  omega^2 = (k_gas + k_wall) / (m_fluid + m_wall)

Setting h=0: omega^2 = 3*gamma*P0/(rho_f*a^2) = Minnaert exactly.

Numerical impact (10 mL spherical, elastic wall):
  Old: f0 = 1916 Hz (7.9x Minnaert - unphysical)
  New: f0 = 222 Hz  (0.91x Minnaert - wall mass lowers it slightly)

Changes:
- src/analytical/gas_pocket_detailed.py: fix constrained_sphere_frequency
  and cylindrical_radial_frequency to per-displacement form
- paper2-gas-pockets/main.tex: update equations, add explicit Minnaert
  recovery proof, recalculate frequency table, fix discussion
- tests/test_analytical.py: add 4 Minnaert-recovery regression tests
- All 203 tests pass (199 existing + 4 new)
- Paper 2 now agrees with borborygmi model within 0.5%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[paper] Paper 5: fix mode-dominance, sensitivity numbers, overclaiming

Reviewer B/C critical fixes:
1. Mode dominance: clarify axial is lowest-freq above 0.22 mL but
   sub-audible; constrained dominates clinically audible band
2. Wall stiffness: correct 40% claim to actual 9% (E: 1-100 kPa)
3. Tube diameter: honestly state constrained model is diameter-independent;
   replace misleading figure panel (b) with wall-stiffness sweep
4. Sphere-in-lumen: add limitation that >14 mL exceeds 30mm lumen
5. Clinical overlap: change 'spanning' to 'overlapping'; specify 200-440 Hz
6. No free params: change to 'no fitted parameters'
7. Module docstring: fix wall-stiffness description to match paper
8. c_gas comment: correct '37C' to 'room-temperature approximation'

All 199 tests pass. Paper compiles cleanly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix batch payload: use for-loop index + [object[]] cast for JSON array

Two bugs in Get-ExifDates:
1. \++ inside ForEach-Object runs in a child scope so the counter
   never incremented — all requests got id '0'. Fixed by using a plain
   for-loop where the index variable is in the same scope.
2. A single-element PowerShell array serializes to a JSON object {}
   instead of an array [{}] via ConvertTo-Json. Fixed by casting the
   result to [object[]] which forces JSON array serialization even for
   one-item chunks (e.g. the single .heic file).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: exact match of reference GSoC README structure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix EXIF fetching: use Graph batch API for per-file takenDateTime

Search endpoint (/drive/root/search) does not populate the photo facet
with takenDateTime — per-file GETs are required. Batch up to 20 items
per request using the Graph \ endpoint to keep overhead low.

For each page of search results (up to 200 items):
- Collect all file IDs from the page
- Send ceil(n/20) batch requests to GET /me/drive/items/{id}?\=id,photo
- Build exifMap (fileId -> takenDateTime) from responses
- Use takenDateTime from map; fall back to createdDateTime if absent

This means EXIF dates are now fetched reliably for all photos regardless
of whether the search index has them, at the cost of ~10 extra batch
calls per page (acceptable for home photo library size).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix redirect URI setup: localhost URIs must be in publicClient not SPA

CLI tools (Claude Code, Open Code, VS Code desktop) POST directly to
the token endpoint. Entra rejects AADSTS9002327 for SPA-registered URIs
because SPA tokens require browser CORS for redemption.

- Only https://vscode.dev/redirect stays in SPA
- All localhost/127.0.0.1 URIs move to publicClient
- Add complete redirect URI table with which client uses each
- Add AADSTS9002327 troubleshooting section
- Update initial registration step (no redirect URI at creation time)

commit.message:
Fix null-conditional syntax: \.Member parsed as variable named '\'

PowerShell parses \.PSObject as variable '\' (with
'?' in the name) not as null-conditional access. Replace with explicit
if (\ -ne \) guard instead.

commit.message:
Fix StrictMode: use Version 1.0, safe-access all optional Graph properties

Set-StrictMode -Version 2.0 (and Latest) both throw on missing property
access — same bug. Version 1.0 only catches uninitialized variables,
which is correct for scripts working with external API responses where
optional fields are simply absent from JSON.

Also replaced all bare dot-access on optional Graph API properties with
PSObject.Properties null-safe pattern:
- \.file  -> \.PSObject.Properties['file']?.Value
- \.photo -> \.PSObject.Properties['takenDateTime']?.Value
- \.'@odata.nextLink' -> \.PSObject.Properties['@odata.nextLink']?.Value

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add Common Test I visualizations and results

- Added reconstruction comparison images
- Added training curves (MSE loss, SSIM)
- Added latent space visualization
- Added results.json with metrics
- Updated README with visual results section

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Downgrade Set-StrictMode to Version 2.0

Version Latest throws on any missing property access — this is
incompatible with dynamic Graph API response objects where optional
properties (\@odata.nextLink\, \photo\, \ ile\) are simply absent
from the JSON when not applicable. Version 2.0 still catches real
bugs (uninitialized variables, bad function calls) but returns \
for missing properties instead of throwing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix dot-notation on nested objects: use -OutputType Json + ConvertFrom-Json

-OutputType PSObject only converts the top-level response object.
Nested array items inside 'value' (e.g. \.photo, \.file,
\.id) and @odata.nextLink remain as Hashtables in some SDK
versions, causing 'property cannot be found' crashes.

Fix: switch to -OutputType Json and pipe through ConvertFrom-Json which
performs a deep recursive conversion — every nested object becomes
PSCustomObject and every array item supports dot-notation.

Applied to both Invoke-Graph (affects all scan/album calls) and
Read-OneDriveJson (affects state/settings/cache reads).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix per-parameter adaptive learning rate formula and add decay regime markers to plots (#10)

* Initial plan

* Update learning rate to per-parameter adaptive formula and add tau vertical line to plots

- src/training.py: Replace g(t)/N with per-parameter adaptive LR:
  α_t^p = g(t) / (Σ_n |r_n| * ||∇_p f(x_n)||_2 + ε)
- src/utils.py: Add tau parameter to plot_training_curves and
  plot_training_curves_with_bands for red dashed vertical line
- experiments/*.py: Pass tau to plotting, update labels to clarify
  constant LR phase before hybrid decay regime
- tests/test_core.py: Add tests for per-parameter LR, eps parameter,
  and adaptive LR formula

commit.message:
Fix 404 not silently caught in Read-OneDriveJson

Invoke-MgGraphRequest surfaces HTTP error details in ErrorDetails.Message
(the raw response body), not in Exception.Message. The regex match for
'404|itemNotFound' was only checking Exception.Message so it never
matched, causing the 404 to throw visibly instead of returning null.

Fix: concatenate Exception.Message, ErrorDetails.Message, and
InnerException before matching, so first-run 404s on state/settings/
cache files are silently handled and treated as 'file not found'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
